name: Snails, Portuguese Style
recipeInstructions:
1. Obtain snails with a diameter of about ½ inch.
2. Starve them for 3 or 4 days to remove any toxic herbs they may have ingested.
3. Wash the snails in running water until all slime is removed.
4. Place the snails in a large pan and cover with water (at least 2 inches or up to 3 inches above the snails).
5. Add all other ingredients: olive oil, dried oregano, laurel leaf, minced garlic, quartered onion, salt, pepper, and piri-piri peppers.
6. Cover the pan tightly and heat the water over a very slow fire so that the snails have time to extend their heads out of their shells.
7. Let it boil slowly for the first hour. Skim off any foam or impurities.
8. Simmer for another hour, skimming again if needed.
9. Serve warm in saucers with a small amount of the cooking liquid. Use toothpicks or pins to extract the snails from their shells.
10. Dip small pieces of bread into the cooking liquid.
11. The dish can be refrigerated and used within a week. Suggested wines include Portuguese Vinho Verde or a dry white wine.
